findings.title: Navigation roots are uneven for docs and AIDE metadata
evidence: docs/ had no top-level README.md before this audit.
.aide/ had no top-level README.md before this audit while command metadata is substantial.
This audit adds concise top-level README pointers; a drift guard remains future work.
findings.impact: New contributors and agents can miss critical operating guidance.
findings.recommended_next_test: Docs link and required index drift guard.
findings.recommended_next_work: Add a docs link and command drift guard so navigation roots stay accurate.
do_not_do: Do not make product maturity claims in navigation indexes.

findings.title: Command metadata can drift across AIDE, docs, scripts, and registry
evidence: .aide/commands/dev.yaml and ci.yaml are manually maintained.
control/inventory/tests/test_registry.json now provides a structured command inventory.
findings.impact: Agents may run stale commands or miss required checks.
findings.recommended_next_test: Command drift guard for registry ids and script paths.
blocked_by: Existing AIDE command files are non-JSON YAML
do_not_do: Do not make optional Rust commands required.
Do not delete existing AIDE metadata without a migration task.

findings.title: Route inventory is static and manually curated
evidence: Route inventory has 89 entries.
Current tests validate structure and local-path policy but do not discover every server route automatically.
findings.impact: Future route additions could be missed by inventory unless tests or review catch them.
findings.recommended_next_test: Route discovery or handler-table comparison when route structure is centralized.
findings.recommended_next_work: Keep manual route inventory explicit and add a drift guard later.
blocked_by: No single central route table yet
do_not_do: Do not redesign server routing in this audit.

findings.title: Source coverage is the dominant usefulness blocker
evidence: The source registry has 6 records.
Search Usefulness Audit v0 reports 43 source_gap queries out of 64.
findings.impact: Eureka cannot yet satisfy many real archive-resolution queries regardless of planner or index quality.
findings.recommended_next_test: Recorded fixture validation for each new source family.
findings.recommended_next_work: Add governed recorded fixtures for one or two high-value query families.
blocked_by: No recorded fixtures for legacy browser releases, drivers, OCR scans, or dead-link snapshots
do_not_do: Do not scrape external search engines.

findings.title: Container, member, and article-level queries need resource fixtures
evidence: Search-usefulness query families include article_inside_scan and package_container_member.
Current decomposition/member behavior is proven only for bounded ZIP fixtures.
findings.impact: Eureka cannot yet prove smallest actionable unit behavior for support media, ISOs, scans, or OCR articles.
findings.recommended_next_test: Hard tests that reject parent-container-only success when a member/article is requested.
findings.recommended_next_work: Add recorded support-media and OCR snippet fixtures before broader decomposition work.
do_not_do: Do not build a broad extractor framework yet.

findings.title: External baseline observations are intentionally pending
evidence: Search Usefulness Audit v0 marks Google, Internet Archive metadata, and Internet Archive full-text baselines as pending_manual_observation for all 64 queries.
No committed external observation records are present.
findings.impact: The repo can compare Eureka's local output dimensions but cannot yet claim observed external wins or losses.
findings.recommended_next_test: Reject observed external records that lack manual evidence metadata.
findings.recommended_next_work: Define a manual baseline observation acceptance process.
blocked_by: Human observation policy not yet accepted
do_not_do: Do not fabricate Google or Internet Archive results.

findings.title: Smallest actionable unit doctrine is only partially proven
evidence: ZIP member listing and readback are implemented for bounded fixtures.
Hard evals for driver inside support media and article inside scanned magazine remain gaps.
findings.impact: Eureka's central promise is not yet proven on the hardest archive-resolution object classes.
findings.recommended_next_test: Member-level hard evals that distinguish parent container from actionable member.
findings.recommended_next_work: Add support-media and scanned-article fixture lanes before expanding behavior.
findings.suggested_milestone: Eval Capability Gap Reduction Pack v0
do_not_do: Do not mark parent containers as satisfied when member-level evidence is required.

findings.title: Compatibility clarity lacks source-rich evidence
evidence: Compatibility seam exists with fixed host profile presets.
Search usefulness reports compatibility_evidence_gap across legacy OS queries.
findings.impact: Legacy software queries need source-backed OS/version evidence before recommendations are trustworthy.
findings.recommended_next_test: Compatibility verdict tests requiring evidence references for known outcomes.
findings.recommended_next_work: Add compatibility evidence fixtures for selected releases and drivers.
do_not_do: Do not invent compatibility from names or dates alone.

findings.title: Identity and disagreement handling needs richer multi-source cases
evidence: Comparison and subject-state seams exist.
Current corpus has few meaningful multi-source disagreement or ambiguity cases.
findings.impact: The repo cannot yet prove durable handling of ambiguous names, version aliases, or source disagreement.
findings.recommended_next_test: Multi-source disagreement fixture with conflicting release metadata.
findings.recommended_next_work: Add recorded fixture pairs that disagree without requiring merge/trust ranking.
do_not_do: Do not introduce trust ranking or fuzzy merge to hide disagreement.

findings.title: Hard eval weakening needs an explicit guard
evidence: Archive-resolution and search-usefulness evals are intentionally hard.
Current tests validate structure and runner output but do not lock every hard expectation against weakening.
findings.impact: Future changes could make evals easier without improving behavior.
findings.recommended_next_test: Snapshot hard query ids, expected current statuses, and expected gap classes.
findings.recommended_next_work: Implement a Hard Test Pack v0 focused on eval truth preservation.
do_not_do: Do not rewrite hard fixtures to look green.

findings.title: Docs link and command drift are under-tested
evidence: README, scripts README, AIDE metadata, and operations docs all reference commands.
No dedicated link/command existence checker exists.
findings.impact: Repo navigation can silently go stale even while runtime tests pass.
findings.recommended_next_work: Check required docs links and script file references.
do_not_do: Do not add external markdown tooling unless explicitly accepted.

findings.title: Rust parity lane is healthy but narrow
evidence: Rust source-registry candidate exists.
Python oracle goldens cover more seams than Rust candidate behavior currently implements.
findings.impact: Rust migration cannot safely replace Python behavior until more seam parity exists.
findings.recommended_next_test: Rust query-planner JSON parity tests against Python oracle goldens.
findings.recommended_next_work: Implement Rust Query Planner Parity Candidate v0 without wiring it into runtime.
findings.suggested_milestone: Rust Query Planner Parity Candidate v0
blocked_by: Cargo may be unavailable in some environments
do_not_do: Do not replace Python runtime paths.
Do not add Rust gateway, CLI, FFI, or services.
